title: carex flava subsp. oedocarpa
intro: Carex flava subsp. oedocarpa, also known as golden sedge, is a perennial plant species within the sedge family (Cyperaceae) characterized by its golden yellow stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. Its health benefits include the potential treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as the reduction of inflammation and improvement of digestion. In herbal preparations, Carex flava subsp. oedocarpa is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
